The Proposal: -
According to the National Forest Policy, 1988, at least 33% of the total geographical area of the country is covered by forests.
It is expected to be forested and tree-lined. Current forest cover and tree cover in the state of Maharashtra
The total 21.25 percent is 16.53 percent and 4.72 percent, respectively. Therefore, the national level goals
That is, if you want to increase the total area of the state by 33%, then the increase is 11.75%.
It has to be done in a green cover. This increase will be driven by climate change in the state.
It will be possible to prevent adverse effects, green cover will reduce soil erosion, water
Prevention of runoff will help water percolate into the soil, increasing the water table. Rural
It will also create employment opportunities in urban areas.
Due to various schemes being implemented by the forest department in the state
Forest cover and tree cover are being improved in a phased manner. However, the Forest Department's new
Due to the limited cultivable area and the limitation of tree plantation on a very large scale in the forest area.
Therefore, the focus is on the available land outside the forest area by giving a more comprehensive form to these efforts.
Afforestation through focused and active participation of government agencies as well as various other agencies.
And the goal of tree cover growth needs to be achieved. This includes the trees of the native species of the state.
It needs to be cultivated. In this regard, Shri. Date chaired by the Chief Minister

In order to be effective, the following strategic measures need to be taken: -
1) To set up a special purpose separate institutional mechanism for undertaking plantation drives in the state.

adoption of the concept.

Setting standard procedures and criteria for selecting native species.
5) Strengthening the supply chain of rotten plants and seeds by empowering nurseries.

A. 10 years to ensure maximum survival rate
Allocate the necessary funds for conservation and preservation for the period.
Joint Forest Management Committee (JFMC), Community Forest Rights Management Committee
(CFRMC), etc., to carry out conservation and conservation works through village level committees and to

Policy, 2026 and utilization of CSR funds as per requirement.
8) Through tertiary mechanisms for outcome-based assessment of tree plantation campaigns.
Third-party verification, digital and satellite-based modern
Operation of the control system.
Considering the above facts, the state has 300 crore tree plantations by 2047.

A special purpose vehicle named "Green Maharashtra Commission."
